response.justification: Candidate has solid generalist software engineering experience and notable geospatial exposure through GISCollective, but the core requirement of modern C++ (or Rust) production experience is largely absent. C/C++ work at National Instruments was brief (~6 months) and over a decade old. Most recent work is in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. The geospatial background and distributed-systems exposure at Sociomantic are relevant adjacent strengths, but the language/stack mismatch is significant for a Senior IC role centered on C++ routing pipelines.
strengths: Geospatial domain experience via GISCollective open-source GIS platform
Distributed/low-latency systems work at Sociomantic handling millions of req/sec
Strong engineering fundamentals: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, testing culture
concerns: Minimal and outdated C++ experience; no Rust experience listed
No navigation/routing systems or map-tile pipeline background
Recent stack is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, not the role's primary languages

key_factors.factor: Lack of modern C++/Rust production experience
